body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
There are 2 diagnostics we aren't able to compute when using CoupledAtmosphere/CoupledRadiativeFluxes because the required variables are not stored: wind speed and net radiation. This PR checks the forcing type and computes the diagnostics correctly based on that. This is done in the same way we handle other diagnostics that are only available in certain cases (e.g. runoff diagnostics).

Closes #1422
title: check for coupled forcing in diagnostic setups

body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
Part of Yuchen's PR #1330

Switches from storing matric potential in `PrescribedGroundConditions` to storing water content. This is a quantity more likely to be available in input from data or users.

Closes #1412

## Content
- [x] store \theta instead of \psi in PrescribedGroundConditions
- [x] convert water content to matrix potential in PlantHydraulics
- [x] confirm standalone canopy results are unchanged

body: ## Purpose This adds the Vaira run we had in the paper - I changed the parameters slightly to obtain a better fit, but note that the parameter changes here are the same as those made 6 months ago for the paper, with the exception of Vcmax25. I decreased that (not surprised it had to change because we had a bug in how we computed photosynthesis before with regards to leaf vs canopy level rates). I had to update the comparison data function so we could get the soil values at more levels. mean(abs.(SW_u_model_monthly .- SW_u_data_monthly)) = 8.753889990968787 mean(abs.(SHF_model_monthly .- SHF_data_monthly)) = 17.06341245068128 mean(abs.(LHF_model_monthly .- LHF_data_monthly)) = 5.643895118148184 mean(abs.(GPP_model_monthly .- GPP_data_monthly)) = 1.725519201393998 ## To-do ## Content ---- - [ ] I have read and checked the items on the review checklist.
title: Vaira plot for paper.

body:
closes #1374, closes #1270

This PR removes the constructor for parameters that takes in `FT` and replace them if they did not exist already with constructor using `toml_dict`. Furthermore, the parameters for the P model is now in the TOML file.

- [x] Check documentation (also see https://github.com/CliMA/ClimaLand.jl/pull/1367#discussion_r2305519956)
title: More usage of ClimaParams
